hero.title: Software engineer, gamer, and cueca dancer.
hero.bio: I’m Samuel, a Chilean software engineer based in Santiago. I like building web products and, lately, getting into agentic AI. I’m also a Bitcoin enthusiast.

about.heading: About
about.p1: I studied Computer Science at Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile and have built software across fintech, foodtech and now micromobility. Today I work remotely from Chile with Forest’s operations team in London.
about.p2: I like studying how things work and keeping up with the latest, from reading programming blogs to following trends in architecture, PWAs, Domain Driven Design and monorepos. I also really enjoy the Vercel ecosystem and have experience building on it. Most of all, I’m interested in bringing AI into how teams work, and I like contributing so that everyone, even non-devs, can make the most of it.

items.description: Building the app behind an e-bike operation in London, the biggest e-bike market in the world, working day to day with the operations team to keep the fleet running.
Frontend development of internal platforms for a crypto arbitrage fintech. Technologies used were Vue.js, Nuxt and Tailwind.
Development of platforms to facilitate the multidisciplinary work of food scientists. Technologies used were Vue.js, Python and GraphQL.
Review of photographic data for the recognition of vehicles and their license plates. Python and React were used for data management.
Implementation of a new notification system in Ruby on Rails and Vue.js with the compensation team.
A mentorship program focused on algorithms, teamwork and software engineering, covering front and production engineering concepts and how to approach technical interviews.

education.heading: Education
education.minor: Minor in Data Science and Analytics

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.body: Have a project or role in mind? I’m open to new opportunities.

project.heading: Project
project.description: An accreditation and document-compliance platform for events in Chile, used by the Festival de Viña del Mar, Latin America’s largest music festival, and by production companies like Bizarro and Street Machine. It handles the paperwork of hundreds of workers with a deliberately simple UX, designed for non-technical users so it works without any training.
